reasoning: The Islamic Republic of Iran has demonstrated significant resilience against domestic protests and international pressure over its 45-year history. Its security apparatus, particularly the IRGC, maintains strong internal cohesion. While regional conflicts and economic woes persist, they are very unlikely to cause a complete collapse of the central government within a single year. Leadership succession (e.g., if Khamenei dies) is explicitly excluded from the resolution criteria. Furthermore, given the very short time window remaining in 2025, if considering a late-2025 forecast date, the probability drops to the absolute minimum. Therefore, I assign a 2% probability, constrained by the lower bound of allowed predictions, heavily relying on the low base rate of entrenched authoritarian collapse.
